item.title: 过去一周的 7 大精选 DEV 帖子 | Top 7 Featured DEV Posts from the Past Week
item.description:
每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和一些编辑策划来汇总前一周的热门帖子。 典型的一周从星期一开始到星期日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

您是否注意到 DEV 上有很多 Web 开发和初学者内容？ 代码审查很难！ 作为初学者，您可能会发现自己茫然地盯着代码，想知道什么是适合建议的，什么是不适合建议的。 从@saminarp的经验中学习并查看这些最佳实践以掌握代码审查的精妙艺术。

构建和部署 Node.js 微服务应用程序

在现代软件开发方面，微服务是最热门的趋势之一。 在本文中， @pavanbelagatti将展示构建微服务应用程序的好处！

玩 console.log() 💿

运行 console.log(console)，您会发现控制台对象还有更多内容。 一分钟模拟 API

好消息：您可以在一分钟内模拟一个 API，而且您不需要编写任何脚本。 撰写您的第一份会议提案

也许你一直在考虑在会议上发言，但你很紧张。 希望@bekahhw的这篇博文能帮助您发现想法和/或克服提交的恐惧。

许多人涌向微服务，因为他们处理的应用程序就像一盘杂乱的意大利面条。 这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 请关注本周的 dev.to 以获取每日内容和讨论……并确保在未来关注本系列。 你可能只是在其中！



原文:

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

Is dev.to for Web Devs and Beginners?

Have you noticed a lot of web dev and beginner content on DEV? @derlin has the data to prove that that’s our bread and butter!

✨Today I Learned: The Subtle Art of Code Reviews 💡✨

Code-reviewing is tough! As a first-timer, you might find yourself staring blankly at the code wondering what is and isn't appropriate to suggest. Learn from @saminarp's experience and check out these best practices to master the subtle art of code reviews.

Build and Deploy a Node.js Microservices Application

When it comes to modern software development, microservices are one of the hottest trends. In this article, @pavanbelagatti will show the benefits of building a microservices app!

Fun with console.log() 💿

Run console.log(console), and you'll see that there's much more to the console object. @lissy93 briefly outlines the top 10 neat tricks you can use to level up your logging experience.

Mock an API in One Minute

Great news: you can mock an API in only one minute and you don't need to write any scripts. @manikbajaj will show you how!

Writing Your First Conference Proposal

Maybe you’ve been thinking about speaking at a conference, but you’re nervous. Hopefully, this blog post from @bekahhw will help you discover an idea and/or overcome the fear of submitting.

Spring Modulith: Have We Reached Modularity Maturity?

Many folks flock to microservices because the application they work on resembles a messy plate of spaghetti. 🍝 @nfrankel contends that if ya have a well-designed application, the pull of microservices isn't so strong.

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!
item.author: michaeltharrington

item.title: 穷人的API | A poor man's API
item.description:
创建一个成熟的 API 需要资源，包括时间和金钱。 您需要考虑模型、设计、REST 原则等，而无需编写一行代码。 大多数时候，您不知道它是否值得：您想要提供最小可行产品并从那里进行迭代。 我想展示如何在不编写一行代码的情况下实现它。

解决方案

该解决方案的主要要求是使用PostgreSQL 数据库。 它是一个成熟的开源 SQL 数据库。

我们没有编写 REST API，而是使用 PostgREST 组件：

数据库中的结构约束和权限决定了 API 端点和操作。

让我们将它应用到一个简单的用例中。 这是我想通过 CRUD API 公开的product表：

请注意，您可以在GitHub 上找到完整的源代码以进行后续操作。

然而，我没有找到任何现成的 Docker 镜像，所以我创建了自己的：

 FROM debian:bookworm-slim #1 ARG POSTGREST_VERSION=v10.1.1 #2 ARG POSTGREST_FILE=postgrest-$POSTGREST_VERSION-linux-static-x64.tar.xz #2 RUN mkdir postgrest WORKDIR postgrest ADD https://github.com/PostgREST/postgrest/releases/download/$POSTGREST_VERSION/$POSTGREST_FILE \ . #3 RUN apt-get update && \ apt-get install -y libpq-dev xz-utils && \ tar xvf $POSTGREST_FILE && \ rm $POSTGREST_FILE #4
  1. 从最新的 Debian 开始
  2. 参数化构建
  3. 获取存档
  4. 安装依赖项并解压

我们可以通过 Docker Compose“部署”架构：

 version : " 3" services : postgrest : build : ./postgrest #1 volumes : - ./postgrest/product.conf:/etc/product.conf:ro #2 ports : - " 3000:3000" entrypoint : [ " /postgrest/postgrest" ] #3 command : [ " /etc/product.conf" ] #4 depends_on : - postgres postgres : image : postgres:15-alpine environment : POSTGRES_PASSWORD : " root" volumes : - ./postgres:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro #5
  1. 构建上面的Dockerfile
  2. 共享配置文件
  3. 运行postgrest可执行文件
  4. 与配置文件
  5. 初始化架构、权限和数据

此时，我们可以查询product表：

 curl localhost:3000/product

我们立即得到结果：

 [{ "id" : 1 , "name" : "Stickers pack" , "description" : "A pack of rad stickers to display on your laptop or wherever you feel like. Show your love for Apache APISIX" , "price" : 0.49 , "hero" : false }, { "id" : 2 , "name" : "Lapel pin" , "description" : "With this \" Powered by Apache APISIX \" lapel pin, support your favorite API Gateway and let everybody know about it." , "price" : 1.49 , "hero" : false }, { "id" : 3 , "name" : "Tee-Shirt" , "description" : "The classic geek product! At a conference, at home, at work, this tee-shirt will be your best friend." , "price" : 9.99 , "hero" : true }]

那是一场快速的胜利！

改进解决方案

尽管该解决方案有效，但仍有很大的改进空间。 例如，数据库用户不能更改数据，但实际上每个人都可以访问它。 对于产品相关的数据来说这可能不是什么大问题，但是对于医疗数据呢？

它的默认行为非常适合开发中的脚手架。 当需要投入生产时，只要您采取预防措施，它也能很好地工作。 我们依靠像 Nginx 这样的反向代理来提供额外的保护。

代替 nginx，我们将从成熟的 API 网关中受益：输入Apache APISIX 。 我们将把它添加到我们的 Docker Compose 中：

 version : " 3" services : apisix : image : apache/apisix:2.15.0-alpine #1 volumes : - ./apisix/config.yml:/usr/local/apisix/conf/config.yaml:ro ports : - " 9080:9080" restart : always depends_on : - etcd - postgrest etcd : image : bitnami/etcd:3.5.2 #2 environment : ETCD_ENABLE_V2 : " true" ALLOW_NONE_AUTHENTICATION : " yes" ETCD_ADVERTISE_CLIENT_URLS : " http://0.0.0.0:2397" ETCD_LISTEN_CLIENT_URLS : " http://0.0.0.0:2397"
  1. 使用 Apache APISIX
  2. 我们将首先配置 Apache APISIX 来代理对postgrest的调用：

     curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/upstreams/1 -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d ' #1-2 { "type": "roundrobin", "nodes": { "postgrest:3000": 1 #1-3 } }' curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/routes/1 -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d ' #4 { "uri": "/*", "upstream_id": 1 }'
    1. 应该在其中一个 Docker 节点中运行，因此使用 Docker 映像的名称。 或者，使用localhost但一定要公开端口
    2. 创建可重用的上游
    3. 指向 PostgREST 节点
    4. 创建到创建的上游的路由

    我们现在可以通过 APISIX 查询端点：

     curl localhost:9080/product

    它返回与上面相同的结果。

    我们还没有添加任何东西，但我们已经准备好开始工作了。 让我们首先保护我们的 API 免受DDoS攻击。 为了防止 DDoS，我们将使用一个插件。 我们可以在创建特定路由时或在每条路由上设置插件； 在后一种情况下，这是一个全球规则。 我们希望默认保护每条路由，所以我们将使用一个。

     curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/global_rules/1 -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d ' { "plugins": { "limit-count": { #1 "count": 1, #2 "time_window": 5, #2 "rejected_code": 429 #3 } } }'
    1. 限制为每 5 秒调用 1 次； 用于演示目的
    2. 返回429 Too Many Requests ； 默认为503

    现在，如果我们执行太多请求，Apache APISIX 会保护上游：

     curl localhost:9080/product
     <html> <head><title> 429 Too Many Requests </title></head> <body> <center><h1> 429 Too Many Requests </h1></center> <hr><center> openresty </center> </body> </html>

    每条路线授权

    因此，我们有两条路线： /用于 Open API 规范和/product用于产品。 假设我们想要禁止未经授权的人访问我们的数据：普通用户可以访问产品，而管理员用户可以访问 Open API 规范和产品。

    我们将使用最简单的一种， key-auth 。 它依赖于消费者抽象。 以下是创建消费者的方法：

     curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/consumers -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d ' #1 { "username": "admin", #2 "plugins": { "key-auth": { "key": "admin" #3 } } }'
    1. 创建一个新的消费者
    2. 消费者姓名
    3. 消费者关键价值

    我们对 consumer user和 key user做同样的事情。 现在，我们可以创建一个专用路由并对其进行配置，以便只有来自admin的请求通过：

     curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/routes -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X POST -d ' #1 { "uri": "/", "upstream_id": 1, "plugins": { "key-auth": {}, #2 "consumer-restriction": { #2 "whitelist": [ "admin" ] #3 } } }'
    1. 创建新路线
    2. 使用key-auth和consumer-restriction插件
    3. 只有经过admin身份验证的请求才能调用该路由

    让我们尝试以下操作：

    它不起作用，因为我们没有通过 API 密钥标头进行身份验证。

     { "message" : "Missing API key found in request" }

    curl -H "apikey: user" localhost:9080

    它不起作用，因为我们已通过user身份验证，但该路由未授权给user而是授权给admin 。

     { "message" : "The consumer_name is forbidden." }

    curl -H "apikey: admin" localhost:9080

    这一次，它按预期返回了 Open API 规范。

    监控

    任何软件系统的一个被低估的功能是监控。 一旦在生产中部署任何组件，就必须监控其运行状况。 如今，许多服务都可用于监控。 我们将使用Prometheus ，因为它是开源的、久经考验且应用广泛的。 为了显示数据，出于同样的原因，我们将依赖Grafana 。 让我们将组件添加到 Docker Compose 文件中：

     version : " 3" services : prometheus : image : prom/prometheus:v2.40.1 #1 volumes : - ./prometheus/prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml #2 depends_on : - apisix grafana : image : grafana/grafana:8.5.15 #3 volumes : - ./grafana/provisioning:/etc/grafana/provisioning #4 - ./grafana/dashboards:/var/lib/grafana/dashboards #4 - ./grafana/config/grafana.ini:/etc/grafana/grafana.ini #4-5 ports : - " 3001:3001" depends_on : - prometheus
    1. 普罗米修斯形象
    2. 用于抓取 Apache APISIX 的 Prometheus 配置。 在此处查看完整文件
    3. 格拉法纳配置。 其中大部分来自APISIX 提供的配置。
    4. 将默认端口从3000更改为3001以避免与 PostgREST 服务冲突

    一旦监控基础设施到位，我们只需要指示 APISIX 以 Prometheus 期望的格式提供数据。 我们可以通过配置和一个新的全局规则来实现它：

     plugin_attr : prometheus : export_addr : ip : " 0.0.0.0" #1 port : 9091 #2
    1. 绑定到任何地址
    2. 绑定到端口9091 。 我们可以创建全局规则：

       curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/global_rules/2 -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d ' { "plugins": { "prometheus": {} } }'

      发送几个查询并打开 Grafana 仪表板。 它看起来应该类似于：

      结论

      创建一个成熟的 REST(ful) API 是一项巨大的投资。 通过 PostgREST 在 CRUD API 中公开数据库，可以快速测试简单的 API。 然而，这样的架构不适合生产使用。

      要修复它，您需要在 PostgREST 前面设置一个外观，一个反向代理，或者更好的 API 网关。 有了它，您可以以低成本快速验证您的 API 需求。

      锦上添花：当您验证了需求后，您可以保留现有外观并用您定制开发的 API 替换 PostgREST。

      源代码在 GitHub 上可用：

      更进一步：

      • 最初于 2022 年 11 月 20日发表于A Java Geek



原文:

Creating a full-fledged API requires resources, both time and money. You need to think about the model, the design, the REST principles, etc., without writing a single line of code. Most of the time, you don't know whether it's worth it: you'd like to offer a Minimum Viable Product and iterate from there. I want to show how you can achieve it without writing a single line of code.

The solution

The main requirement of the solution is to use the PostgreSQL database. It's a well-established Open Source SQL database.

Instead of writing our REST API, we use the PostgREST component:

PostgREST is a standalone web server that turns your PostgreSQL database directly into a RESTful API. The structural constraints and permissions in the database determine the API endpoints and operations.

-- PostgREST

Let's apply it to a simple use case. Here's a product table that I want to expose via a CRUD API:

Note that you can find the whole source code on GitHub to follow along.

PostgREST's Getting Started guide is pretty complete and works out of the box. Yet, I didn't find any ready-made Docker image, so I created my own:

FROM debian:bookworm-slim #1 ARG POSTGREST_VERSION=v10.1.1 #2
ARG POSTGREST_FILE=postgrest-$POSTGREST_VERSION-linux-static-x64.tar.xz #2 RUN mkdir postgrest WORKDIR postgrest ADD https://github.com/PostgREST/postgrest/releases/download/$POSTGREST_VERSION/$POSTGREST_FILE \ . #3 RUN apt-get update && \
 apt-get install -y libpq-dev xz-utils && \
 tar xvf $POSTGREST_FILE && \
 rm $POSTGREST_FILE #4
  1. Start from the latest Debian
  2. Parameterize the build
  3. Get the archive
  4. Install dependencies and unarchive

The Docker image contains a postgrest executable in the /postgrest folder. We can "deploy" the architecture via Docker Compose:

version: "3"
services: postgrest: build: ./postgrest #1 volumes: - ./postgrest/product.conf:/etc/product.conf:ro #2 ports: - "3000:3000" entrypoint: ["/postgrest/postgrest"] #3 command: ["/etc/product.conf"] #4 depends_on: - postgres postgres: image: postgres:15-alpine environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: "root" volumes: - ./postgres:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro #5
  1. Build the above Dockerfile
  2. Share the configuration file
  3. Run the postgrest executable
  4. With the configuration file
  5. Initialize the schema, the permissions, and the data

At this point, we can query the product table:

curl localhost:3000/product

We immediately get the results:

[{"id":1,"name":"Stickers pack","description":"A pack of rad stickers to display on your laptop or wherever you feel like. Show your love for Apache APISIX","price":0.49,"hero":false}, {"id":2,"name":"Lapel pin","description":"With this \"Powered by Apache APISIX\" lapel pin, support your favorite API Gateway and let everybody know about it.","price":1.49,"hero":false}, {"id":3,"name":"Tee-Shirt","description":"The classic geek product! At a conference, at home, at work, this tee-shirt will be your best friend.","price":9.99,"hero":true}]

That was a quick win!

Improving the solution

Though the solution works, it has a lot of room for improvement. For example, the database user cannot change the data, but everybody can actually access it. It might not be a big issue for product-related data, but what about medical data?

The PostgREST documentation is aware of it and explicitly advises using a reverse proxy:

PostgREST is a fast way to construct a RESTful API. Its default behavior is great for scaffolding in development. When it’s time to go to production it works great too, as long as you take precautions. PostgREST is a small sharp tool that focuses on performing the API-to-database mapping. We rely on a reverse proxy like Nginx for additional safeguards.

-- Hardening PostgREST

Instead of nginx, we would benefit from a full-fledged API Gateway: enters Apache APISIX. We shall add it to our Docker Compose:

version: "3"
services: apisix: image: apache/apisix:2.15.0-alpine #1 volumes: - ./apisix/config.yml:/usr/local/apisix/conf/config.yaml:ro ports: - "9080:9080" restart: always depends_on: - etcd - postgrest etcd: image: bitnami/etcd:3.5.2 #2 environment: ETCD_ENABLE_V2: "true" ALLOW_NONE_AUTHENTICATION: "yes" ETCD_ADVERTISE_CLIENT_URLS: "http://0.0.0.0:2397" ETCD_LISTEN_CLIENT_URLS: "http://0.0.0.0:2397"
  1. Use Apache APISIX
  2. APISIX stores its configuration in etcd

We shall first configure Apache APISIX to proxy calls to postgrest:

curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/upstreams/1 -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d ' #1-2
{ "type": "roundrobin", "nodes": { "postgrest:3000": 1 #1-3 }
}' curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/routes/1 -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d ' #4
{ "uri": "/*", "upstream_id": 1
}'
  1. Should be run in one of the Docker nodes, so use the Docker image's name. Alternatively, use localhost but be sure to expose the ports
  2. Create a reusable upstream
  3. Point to the PostgREST node
  4. Create a route to the created upstream

We can now query the endpoint via APISIX:

curl localhost:9080/product

It returns the same result as above.

DDoS protection

We haven't added anything, but we're ready to start the work. Let's first protect our API from DDoS attacks. Apache APISIX is designed around a plugin architecture. To protect from DDoS, we shall use a plugin. We can set plugins on a specific route when it's created or on every route; in the latter case, it's a global rule. We want to protect every route by default, so we shall use one.

curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/global_rules/1 -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d '
{ "plugins": { "limit-count": { #1 "count": 1, #2 "time_window": 5, #2 "rejected_code": 429 #3 } }
}'
  1. limit-count limits the number of calls in a time window
  2. Limit to 1 call per 5 seconds; it's for demo purposes
  3. Return 429 Too Many Requests; the default is 503

Now, if we execute too many requests, Apache APISIX protects the upstream:

curl localhost:9080/product

<html>
<head><title>429 Too Many Requests</title></head>
<body>
<center><h1>429 Too Many Requests</h1></center>
<hr><center>openresty</center>
</body>
</html>

Per-route authorization

PostgREST also offers an Open API endpoint at the root. We thus have two routes: / for the Open API spec and /product for the products. Suppose we want to disallow unauthorized people to access our data: Regular users can access products, while admin users can access both the Open API spec and products.

APISIX offers several authentication methods. We will use the simplest one possible, key-auth. It relies on the Consumer abstraction. key-auth requires a specific header: the plugin does a reverse lookup on the value and finds the consumer whose key corresponds.

Here's how to create a consumer:

curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/consumers -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d ' #1
{ "username": "admin", #2 "plugins": { "key-auth": { "key": "admin" #3 } }
}'
  1. Create a new consumer
  2. Consumer's name
  3. Consumer's key value

We do the same with consumer user and key user. Now, we can create a dedicated route and configure it so that only requests from admin pass through:

curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/routes -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X POST -d ' #1
{ "uri": "/", "upstream_id": 1, "plugins": { "key-auth": {}, #2 "consumer-restriction": { #2 "whitelist": [ "admin" ] #3 } }
}'
  1. Create a new route
  2. Use the key-auth and consumer-restriction plugins
  3. Only admin-authenticated requests can call the route

Let's try the following:

It doesn't work as we are not authenticated via an API key header.

{"message":"Missing API key found in request"}

curl -H "apikey: user" localhost:9080

It doesn't work as we are authenticated as user, but the route is not authorized for user but for admin.

{"message":"The consumer_name is forbidden."}

curl -H "apikey: admin" localhost:9080

This time, it returns the Open API spec as expected.

Monitoring

A much-undervalued feature of any software system is monitoring. As soon as you deploy any component in production, you must monitor its health. Nowadays, many services are available to monitor. We will use Prometheus as it's Open Source, battle-proven, and widespread. To display the data, we will rely on Grafana for the same reasons. Let's add the components to the Docker Compose file:

version: "3"
services: prometheus: image: prom/prometheus:v2.40.1 #1 volumes: - ./prometheus/prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml #2 depends_on: - apisix grafana: image: grafana/grafana:8.5.15 #3 volumes: - ./grafana/provisioning:/etc/grafana/provisioning #4 - ./grafana/dashboards:/var/lib/grafana/dashboards #4 - ./grafana/config/grafana.ini:/etc/grafana/grafana.ini #4-5 ports: - "3001:3001" depends_on: - prometheus
  1. Prometheus image
  2. Prometheus configuration to scrape Apache APISIX. See the full file here
  3. Grafana image
  4. Grafana configuration. Most of it comes from the configuration provided by APISIX.
  5. Change the default port from 3000 to 3001 to avoid conflict with the PostgREST service

Once the monitoring infrastructure is in place, we only need to instruct APISIX to provide the data in a format that Prometheus expects. We can achieve it through configuration and a new global rule:

plugin_attr: prometheus: export_addr: ip: "0.0.0.0" #1 port: 9091 #2
  1. Bind to any address
  2. Bind to port 9091. Prometheus metrics are available on http://apisix:9091/apisix/prometheus/metrics on the Docker network

We can create the global rule:

curl http://apisix:9080/apisix/admin/global_rules/2 -H 'X-API-KEY: 123xyz' -X PUT -d '
{ "plugins": { "prometheus": {} }
}'

Send a couple of queries and open the Grafana dashboard. It should look similar to this:

Conclusion

Creating a full-fledged REST(ful) API is a huge investment. One can quickly test a simple API by exposing one's database in a CRUD API via PostgREST. However, such an architecture is not fit for production usage.

To fix it, you need to set a façade in front of PostgREST, a reverse proxy, or even better, an API Gateway. Apache APISIX offers a wide range of features, from authorization to monitoring. With it, you can quickly validate your API requirements at a low cost.

The icing on the cake: when you've validated the requirements, you can keep the existing façade and replace PostgREST with your custom-developed API.

The source code is available on GitHub:

To go further:

  • PostgREST
  • Getting started with Apache APISIX
  • Apache APISIX plugins

Originally published at A Java Geek on November 20th, 2022
item.author: nfrankel

item.title: 兴奋又有点紧张 | Excited and a bit Nervous
item.description:
各位编码员和爱好者们，你们好，

这是我在这个充满专业人士和像我这样的初学者的社区中发表的第一篇文章。
我很高兴能充满动力地踏入这个新世界，并开发和创建艺术网络应用程序以及移动应用程序。 我的目标是提升用户体验的细节水平，同时提高工作效率。

那么许多人可能会问为什么紧张？

最近科技领域的裁员和冻结招聘的广泛消息让我开始思考我是否能够找到一份初学者级别的工作。 尽管我很乐观，因为在黑夜结束时，太阳会升起。 有了这个，我期待着以最大的决心迎接这一挑战，努力进入专业氛围，并从我的前辈那里获得急需的经验。

如果你们能分享你们的经验和动力或指导像我这样的学生找到他们的第一份工作/实习，我会很高兴！

原文:

Hello fellow Coders and Enthusiasts,

This is the marking of my first post into this community filled with professionals as well as beginners like me.
I am very excited to step into this new world with motivation and to develop and create artistic web-apps as well as mobile apps. I aim to elevate the level of details in user experience as well as to boost the productivity while at it.

Why Nervous then many may ask?

This recent lay-offs in the tech space and the widespread news of freezing hiring has got me thinking whether I would be able to land a beginner-level job. Although I am very optimistic as at the end of the night, sun rises. With this I look forward to approaching this challenges with utmost determination and strive to enter the professional atmosphere and gain much needed experience from my seniors.

Would love if you guys can share your experiences and motivation or guide students like me towards landing their first job/internships!

LinkedIn Open to Connect

Davide de Paolis - Nov 21

Full Stack Vault - Nov 21

item.title: 跳过 E2E 测试 | Skipping E2E tests
item.description:
泰穆尔列夫逊

发表于作为我必须为我的课程做的提交之一是对某个存储库的贡献。 我发现我的一个同学在chunk repository 上工作，所以我决定看看他们有什么样的问题，并找到一个似乎适合我的问题。

概述

问题本身是关于将一​​些 e2e 测试放在一些代码块上，这些代码块将在 CI 上跳过，因为如果你只是在 CI 上为每个 PR 运行这些测试，这些测试不会提供任何额外的价值，但可能对某些开发人员有用一些案例。

执行

存储库维护者建议我将他们不想运行的测试放在“跳过块”中。 事实证明，您可以使用desribe.skip来完成。 我以前不知道这是一件事，所以这是我从这项任务中得到的许多“收获”。

公关

完成后我创建了PR ，现在我等待维护者查看它并希望它能尽快合并。

一旦暂停，myrfion 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，myrfion 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，myrfion 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 myrfion 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 myrfion 的操作：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 myrfion 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Tymur Levtsun

Posted on Nov 24

As one of the submissions I had to do for my course was a contribution to some repository. I found one of my classmates worked on the chunk repository so I decided to take a look at what kind of issues they have and found one that seemed to be suitable for me.

Overview

The issueitself was about putting some e2e tests to some code block that gonna be skipped on the CI, because these tests don't provide any additional value if you just run them on CI for each PR, but might be useful for some developers in some cases.

Implementation

The repository maintainer recommended me to put tests that they don't want to run in "skip block". It turned out that you can do it using desribe.skip. I didn't know that it was a thing before so it was my many "take-away" from this task.

PR

When I was done I created the PR and now I wait for maintainers to take a look at it and hope it gonna be merged soon.

item.title: 虚拟证明：回调函数 | Dummy Proof: Callback Functions
item.description:
什么是回调函数？

  • 作为函数内部参数传递的函数，稍后将执行

什么时候使用回调？

  • 您可以在异步函数中使用回调，其中一个函数必须等待另一个函数调用。

常规功能：

 function name(argument){ //do something }

回调函数：

 //Defining functions function prepare(ingredients, callback) { console.log("Preparing " + ingredients); callback(); } //callback function function chop(){ console.log("Chopping"); } //Calling the function prepare("onions and garlic", chop);
 function createQuote(quote, callback){ var myQuote = "Like I always say, " + quote; callback(myQuote); // 2 } function logQuote(quote){ console.log(quote); } createQuote("eat your vegetables!", logQuote); // 1 // Result in console: // Like I always say, eat your vegetables!

有关回调函数的知识：

  • 它是一个作为参数传递给另一个函数的函数
  • 不必在参数中命名为“回调”
  • 此函数称为高阶函数，其中包含回调函数何时执行的逻辑
  • 回调函数可以是匿名函数

资源：

这篇帖子在 2020 年的 DEV 上火了：

作为 JavaScript 开发者，我们通常不必自己处理编译器。 然而，了解 JavaScript 引擎的基础知识并了解它如何处理我们对人类友好的 JS 代码并将其转化为机器可以理解的代码绝对是一件好事！ 编码愉快！

一旦暂停，jennman 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，jennman 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jennman 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 jennman 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

What is a callback function?

  • A function passed as an argument inside of a function that will be executed later

When to use a callback ?

  • You can use callbacks in asynchronous functions, where one function has to wait for another function to invoke.

Regular Function :

function name(argument){
   //do something 
}

Callback Function :

//Defining functions
function prepare(ingredients, callback) {
       console.log("Preparing " + ingredients);
       callback();
}

//callback function
function chop(){
       console.log("Chopping");
}

//Calling the function
prepare("onions and garlic", chop);

function createQuote(quote, callback){ 
  var myQuote = "Like I always say, " + quote;
  callback(myQuote); // 2
}

function logQuote(quote){
  console.log(quote);
}

createQuote("eat your vegetables!", logQuote); // 1

// Result in console: 
// Like I always say, eat your vegetables!

Things to know about callback functions :

  • It's a function that is passed as an argument to another function
  • Does not have to be named 'callback' in the argument
  • This function is called a higher-order function which contains the logic for when the callback function gets executed
  • JavaScript has anonymous functions which lets you declare and execute functions without naming them.
  • Callback functions can be anonymous functions

Resources:

This post blew up on DEV in 2020:

🚀⚙️ JavaScript Visualized: the JavaScript Engine

As JavaScript devs, we usually don't have to deal with compilers ourselves. However, it's definitely good to know the basics of the JavaScript engine and see how it handles our human-friendly JS code, and turns it into something machines understand! 🥳

Happy coding!

Once suspended, jennman will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, jennman will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by jennman will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If jennman is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag jennman:

jennman consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging jennman will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: jennman

item.title: 利用 Monokle 的 OPA 安全实践 | Leverage OPA Security Practices with Monokle
item.description:
在Monokle ，我们致力于帮助像您这样的开发人员在使用 Kubernetes 时提高工作效率。 该任务的很大一部分是帮助您在部署过程的早期花费更少的时间调试和修复常见错误。

您知道类型——缩进、拼写错误、拼写错误的资源名称、来自 Helm 或 Kustomize 的无效输出等等。 借助 Monokle，我们在实现让您的 Kubernetes 开发体验愉快的使命的过程中，通过检查您部署前工作流程中的另一个烦恼，帮助立即解决这些痛点。

但是，当我们朝着使配置任务更易于管理的目标努力时，我们非常清楚并非所有错误都是简单的烦恼。 您的组织可能会维护围绕云成本、安全性和数据隐私等问题制定的特定政策和最佳实践。 这些可能来自您的 CTO 牵头的内部讨论或使用来自云原生计算基金会 (CNCF) 的开源验证器工具的云原生最佳实践。

这些规则（和相关的错误）值得您的关注和时间，但这并不意味着策略的执行必须成为您生产力的巨大负担。

我们相信在部署之前验证您解决复杂错误的工作，这样您就可以减少修复它们的时间。 因此，在我们的Monokle 1.7.0版本中，我们添加了对OPA (OPA) 的支持，以自动验证、识别和修复关键任务 Kubernetes 错误。

什么是开放策略代理？

相反，您使用要应用的规则和要测试的代码查询 OPA，它会为您提供通过/失败结果。

它们可以涵盖“简单”的规则，例如要求标签到复杂的指令，例如安全性、云成本和围绕可用性的最佳实践。

我们将自动 OPA 验证直接添加到 Monokle 中，以简化您的开发工作流程。 当您最终准备好部署到您的集群时，不再需要上下文切换或担心您是否违反了云原生最佳实践。

我们实施了由Aqua Security 的 DefSec 团队开发的规则——我们感谢他们的辛勤工作和 OPA 专业知识，以帮助 Monokle 用户启动并运行 OPA。

完整的规则列表可以在我们的文档中找到，但这里有一些示例及其不同严重性级别：

  • 如何通过 Kubernetes 配置文件启用和使用 OPA

如果您还没有 Monokle，请安装桌面应用程序！ 我们支持 macOS、Windows 和 Linux，只需点击几下即可在 Monokle 中查看所有 Kubernetes 资源。

启用 OPA 政策

要将 OPA 集成到您的工作流中，请单击左侧导航菜单上的 *Validate Your Resources* 按钮开始应用和查看 OPA 规则，然后单击 Open Policy Agent 标题下的 *Configure* 按钮以查看可用 rego 列表政策。

我们建议您使用 *Enable all* 按钮将它们全部启用，然后禁用那些引起太多麻烦或不符合您团队策略的那些。 您可以按策略 ID 或严重性对策略进行排序，以定位您的搜索并启用与您的组织最相关的策略。 只需单击任何策略旁边的切换按钮即可启用或禁用它。

根据 OPA 政策验证您的清单和资源

启用策略后，Monokle 开始在后台验证所有配置文件和集群资源——无需启用或配置任何其他内容。

当您开发普通清单时，Monokle 会用红色错误图标标记违反给定策略的代码，就像它对前面提到的“更简单”语法错误所做的那样。 将鼠标悬停在该图标上可查看您违反了哪些 OPA 政策，以及有关修复您的清单的建议。

当您进行更改时，Monokle 会不断使用您的代码查询 OPA，直到您满足组织的最佳实践，然后噗——错误实时消失！

但是 Monokle 将用例更进一步。 由于 OPA 验证也适用于 Helm/Kustomize 输出，因此您可以在尝试部署任何生成的配置文件、对象或资源之前测试这些输出。 现在您可以直接在 Monokle 中看到 OPA 验证错误，您不必使用更多的开发工具，不必浪费时间部署最终不符合组织标准的代码，也不必为回滚生产部署而烦恼。 我们正在不间断地听取用户反馈，因为我们向 Monokle 添加了新的特性和功能，我们的愿景是将您使用 YAML 的时间减少到最少，这样您就可以回到您最喜欢的事情上，编码！

试用 Monokle，将我们的新版本直接发布到您的桌面。 下载macOS、Linux 和 Windows。 它是完全免费和开源的，具有MIT 许可证。

详细了解如何在 Monokle中创建YAML 清单，一旦您花了一些时间利用 Monokle 来验证和修复您的错误（无论是语法还是 OPA 相关），我们邀请您访问我们的Discord 服务器并向我们提供反馈。

我们的工程师、DevRel 和产品团队都活跃在这个领域，并乐于讨论实施更多 OPA 政策、让您为您的组织构建自定义插件的新选项，或者我们可以帮助您解决预部署工作流程中的其他难题.



原文:

At Monokle, we care about helping developers like you be more productive while working with Kubernetes. A big part of that mission is helping you spend far less time debugging and fixing common errors early in the deployment process.

You know the type—indentation, typos, misspelled resource names, invalid output from Helm or Kustomize to name a few. With Monokle, we help resolve these pain points straight away by checking off one more annoyance in your pre-deployment workflow on our way to achieve our mission of making your Kubernetes development experience joyful.

But as we work toward our goal of making configuration tasks easier to manage, we are very aware that not all errors are simple annoyances. Your organization likely maintains specific policies and best practices built around issues like cloud costs, security, and data privacy. These might come from internal discussions spearheaded by your CTO or cloud native best practices using an open-source validator tool from the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF).

These rules (and associated errors) are worthy of your attention and time, but that doesn’t mean that policy enforcement has to be an enormous burden on your productivity.

We believe in validating your work around complex errors before deploying so you spend less time fixing them. So in our Monokle 1.7.0 release we added support forOPA (OPA) to automate how you validate, identify, and fix mission-critical Kubernetes errors.

What is Open Policy Agent?

Open Policy Agent, also referred to as OPA, is an open source general purpose policy engine for building specific policy enforcement across an organization's development stack.

OPA policy allows Kubernetes development teams to define rules on exactly how their systems should behave, but without hardcoding those rules directly into the code itself. Instead, you query OPA with the rules you’re looking to apply and the code you want tested, and it gives you a pass/fail result.

OPA-driven policy decisions are entirely up to your organization. They can cover “simple” rules like requiring labels to complex directives like security, cloud costs, and best practices around availability.

What OPA rules does Monokle include?

We added automatic OPA validation directly into Monokle to keep your development workflows streamlined. No more context switching or worrying about whether you’re violating a cloud native best practice when you’re finally ready to deploy to your cluster.

We implemented rules developed by the DefSec team at Aqua Security—we’re grateful for their hard work and OPA expertise to help Monokle users get up and running with OPA.

The full list of rules can be found in our documentation, but here are some examples and their of various levels of severity:

  • SYS\_ADMIN capability added: Processes in your containers can use root-like privileges.
  • hostPath volume mounted with docker.sock: Your container can have full root access to its host system.
  • Accessto host network: Processes in the pod to communicate with processes bound to the host's loopback adapter.
  • CPU notlimited: Can create resource exhaustion during a DDoS attack.

How to enable and use OPA with your Kubernetes configuration files

If you don’t have Monokle yet, install the desktop app! We support macOS, Windows, and Linux, and it only takes a few clicks to see all your Kubernetes resources in Monokle.

Enable OPA policies

Monokle’s OPA features work with your vanilla Kubernetes manifests, the output from Helm charts or Kustomizations, and cluster resources. To integrate OPA into your workflows, start applying and viewing OPA rules by clicking the *Validate Your Resources* button located on the left-hand navigation menu, then the *Configure* button under the Open Policy Agent heading to view the list of available rego policies.

We recommend you enable them all with the *Enable all*button, and then disable those that cause too much headache or don’t work with your team’s policies. You can sort policies by their ID or severity to target your search and enable those most relevant to your organization. Just click the toggle button next to any policy to enable or disable it.

Validate your manifests and resources against OPA policies

Once you’ve enabled a policy, Monokle gets started validating all configuration files and cluster resources in the background—there’s nothing else to enable or configure.

As you develop a vanilla manifest, Monokle marks code that violates a given policy with a red error icon, just as it does with the "simpler" syntax errors mentioned earlier. Hover over that icon to see what OPA policy you’re violating, along with advice on fixing your manifest.

As you make changes, Monokle keeps querying OPA with your code until you meet your organization’s best practices, and poof—the error disappears in real-time!

But Monokle takes the use cases even further. Since OPA validation works with Helm/Kustomize output too, you can test those outputs before you try deploying any generated config files, objects, or resources. Monokle also lets you inspect your existing clusters for OPA errors, which helps you retroactively fix invalid manifests you deployed before implementing OPA policies or having the right tool to identify them quickly and easily.

Now that you can see OPA validation errors directly in Monokle, you don’t have to juggle more development tools, waste time deploying code that ultimately doesn’t meet your organization’s standards, or fret over rolling back a production deployment. Monokle’s OPA capabilities help reduce complexity of pre deployment configuration tasks and maybe even makes working with K8s objects and resources fun—well, easier for sure!

What’s next with Kubernetes configuration workflows?

We’re working non-stop listening to user feedback as we add new features and capabilities to Monokle with the vision of reducing your time working with YAML to a minimum so you can get back to what you enjoy most, coding!

Try Monokle and get our new releases directly to your desktop. Download for macOS, Linux, and Windows. It’s entirely free and open-source complete with an MIT license.

Learn more about how to createYAML Manifestsin Monokle and once you’ve spent some time leveraging Monokle to validate and fix your errors—whether syntax or OPA related—we invite you to stop by our Discord Server to give us feedback.

Our engineers, DevRel, and product team are all active in this space and are happy to discuss implementing more OPA policies, new options for letting you build custom plugins for your organization, or other pains that we can help you resolve in your pre deployment workflows.
item.author: cortney

item.title: 初学者 JavaScript 项目：如何反转句子并将其显示在屏幕上 | Beginner JavaScript Project: How to reverse a sentence and display it on screen
item.description:
练习 JavaScript 技能的最佳方式是构建小型项目。 在这篇文章中，我们将构建一个接受句子并将其反转的小应用程序。

我们将在 CodePen 中构建这个项目。 它是免费使用的，您需要先 创建一个帐户，然后才能开始使用。 这是您需要分叉的CodePen 起始代码，它包含所有 HTML 和 CSS。

先决条件
所有 HTML 和 CSS 都已为您提供，我们不会在本教程中涵盖任何内容。 本教程假定您具备 HTML 和 CSS 的基本应用知识。 如果您有兴趣学习 HTML 和 CSS，那么我建议您学习freeCodeCamp 的响应式网页设计课程。

目录

反向句子项目背后的基本功能

当您打开CodePen 起始代码时，您应该会看到这个结果。

我们想要构建一个应用程序，用户输入一个句子并单击“反转句子”按钮，反转的句子就会显示在屏幕上。


如果用户在没有提供输入的情况下单击“反向句子”按钮，则会显示一个警告，要求他们提供一个句子。

涵盖的主题

以下是该项目涵盖的主题的完整列表。 您可以单击任何主题，它将带您到一篇文章或 MDN 链接以了解更多信息。

使用 JavaScript 访问 HTML 元素

我们的应用程序具有我们需要在 JavaScript 文件中访问的三个主要 HTML 元素。 我们需要访问包含用户句子值的输入。 我们需要访问按钮，单击该按钮将用于显示结果。 我们还需要访问将显示结果的空段落元素。

有几种方法可以使用 JavaScript 访问元素。 在这个项目中，我们将使用getElementById方法。 此方法将访问与我们指定的 id 匹配的 HTML 元素。 由于 id 名称在 HTML 文档中必须是唯一的，因此我们可以确定我们正在返回正确的元素。

以下是getElementById方法的基本语法：

 document . getElementById ( id );

让我们首先访问id为user-sentence的input 。

 document . getElementById ( " user-sentence " );

我们希望能够将其分配给一个变量，以便稍后在我们的程序中使用它。 我们将创建一个名为userSentence的const变量，并将其分配给document.getElementById("user-sentence") 。 我们在这里使用了const关键字，因为这个const变量不会被重新赋值。

 const userSentence = document . getElementById ( " user-sentence " );

如果您对这个document.getElementById("user-sentence")返回的内容感到好奇，那么我建议您像这样使用console.log ：

 const userSentence = document . getElementById ( " user-sentence " ); console . log ( userSentence );

注意：要访问 CodePen 中的控制台，请单击屏幕左下角的控制台按钮

您可以使用console.log将消息打印到控制台输出。 当您需要了解您的代码在做什么时，此方法会派上用场。

现在我们了解了如何使用console.log ，我们可以从我们的程序中删除该行。

接下来的步骤是访问id为reversed-str-result的段落元素。 我们可以再次使用getElementById方法并将其分配给一个名为reversedStrElement的变量。

 const reversedStrElement = document . getElementById ( " reversed-str-result " );

我们需要访问的最后一个元素是id为reverse-str-btn的按钮。 我们可以再次使用getElementById方法并将其分配给一个名为reversedStrBtn的变量。

 const reversedStrBtn = document . getElementById ( " reverse-str-btn " );

创建反句按钮功能

当用户点击“反转句子”按钮时，我们想要在屏幕上显示反转的句子。 我们需要从使用addEventListener方法开始。

向 Reverse Sentence 按钮添加事件侦听器

我们正在侦听的事件是 click 事件，表示用户何时单击按钮。

在const变量下面，让我们向reversedStrBtn添加一个事件监听器。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { // the rest of our functionality will go here });

我们可以通过添加一个console.log来测试它，它将打印消息“这个按钮被点击”。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { console . log ( " This button was clicked " ); });

打开控制台并单击按钮。 您应该会看到以下结果：

现在我们知道我们的按钮正在工作，我们可以继续并删除那个console.log 。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { // the rest of our functionality will go here });

从输入元素访问值

我们需要能够获取用户的输入，这样我们才能成功地将其反转并显示在屏幕上。 我们可以通过将.value添加到我们的userSentence变量来实现这一点。

在事件侦听器的函数中，让我们为userSentence.value添加一个console.log 。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { console . log ( userSentence . value ); });

打开您的控制台，输入一个句子，然后单击“反转句子”按钮以查看控制台中的值。

我们可以删除那个console.log并将该用户值分配给一个名为userValue的const变量。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; });

为不提供输入的用户添加警报

在我们开始反转字符串之前，我们需要检查用户是否提供了一个值。

在userValue变量下面，我们需要添加一个if语句。 下面是if语句的基本语法：

 if ( /*condition is true*/ ){ // run this code here }

对于我们的条件，我们要检查用户值是否为空字符串。 在if语句中，假值被强制为假。 我们想使用! 反转布尔值的运算符（例如，true 现在是 false，false 现在是 true）。

这就是我们的if的样子：

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( ! userValue ) { // run this code here if condition is true } });

如果您对! 运算符，那么你可以像这样重写你的if语句：

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( userValue === "" ) { // run this code here if condition is true } });

在if语句中，我们想添加一个提醒，让用户知道他们需要提供一个句子。 在该警报下方，我们需要添加return语句，因为如果用户未提供输入，我们希望退出我们的函数。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( ! userValue ) { alert ( " Please provide a sentence " ); return ; } });

在不提供输入的情况下单击“反转句子”按钮以查看警报。

使用 split、reverse 和 join 方法反转句子

有许多不同的方法可以反转字符串。 在这个项目中，我们将使用三个内置的 JavaScript 方法。

第一步是获取我们的userValue并将其拆分为单个字符。 我们可以使用split方法，它接受一个字符串并创建一个字符数组。

在 split 方法的if语句下面添加一个console.log 。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( ! userValue ) { alert ( " Please provide a sentence " ); return ; } console . log ( userValue . split ( "" )); });

打开控制台，提供输入并单击“Reverse Sentence”按钮以查看控制台结果。

接下来，我们要获取该字符数组并使用reverse方法将其反转。
注意：我在这里使用一种称为链接的方法，用于将多个方法链接在一起

reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( ! userValue ) { alert ( " Please provide a sentence " ); return ; } console . log ( userValue . split ( "" ). reverse ()); });

打开控制台，提供输入并单击“Reverse Sentence”按钮以查看控制台结果。

最后，我们需要将该字符数组转换回字符串。 我们可以使用join方法来完成此操作。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( ! userValue ) { alert ( " Please provide a sentence " ); return ; } console . log ( userValue . split ( "" ). reverse (). join ( "" )); });

打开控制台，提供输入并单击“Reverse Sentence”按钮以查看控制台结果。

我们可以删除console.log并将颠倒的句子分配给一个名为reversedSentence的const变量。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( ! userValue ) { alert ( " Please provide a sentence " ); return ; } const reversedSentence = userValue . split ( "" ). reverse (). join ( "" ); });

显示颠倒的句子并使用模板字符串

现在我们有了颠倒的句子，我们希望能够在屏幕上显示该结果。 我们可以使用所谓的模板字符串（或模板文字）在字符串中注入变量。 模板字符串使用反引号而不是双引号 ("") 或单引号 ('')。

这是我们的模板字符串的样子：

 `Reversed sentence: ${ reversedSentence } ` ;

我们希望该值显示在reversedStrElement中，它代表我们 HTML 文档中的空段落元素。 我们可以使用innerText方法在页面上渲染颠倒的句子。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( ! userValue ) { alert ( " Please provide a sentence " ); return ; } const reversedSentence = userValue . split ( "" ). reverse (). join ( "" ); reversedStrElement . innerText = `Reversed sentence: ${ reversedSentence } ` ; });

结果显示在页面上后重置输入值

当结果显示在页面上时，如果输入的值能被清除就好了。 我们通过为userSentence.value分配一个空字符串来实现这一点。

 reversedStrBtn . addEventListener ( " click " , () => { const userValue = userSentence . value ; if ( ! userValue ) { alert ( " Please provide a sentence " ); return ; } const reversedSentence = userValue . split ( "" ). reverse (). join ( "" ); reversedStrElement . innerText = `Reversed sentence: ${ reversedSentence } ` ; userSentence . value = "" ; });

现在你可以用各种句子测试你的项目，应该会看到相反的结果。

这是我们项目的完整代码。 这是最终的工作结果。

结论

我希望你从这个初学者 JavaScript 项目中学到了很多东西。 有关更多此类 JavaScript 项目，请查看以下文章：

  • 40 JavaScript Projects for Beginners – Easy Ideas to Get Started Coding JS 40 个适合初学者的 JavaScript 项目——开始编码 JS 的简单想法


原文:

The best way to practice your JavaScript skills is to build small projects. In this article we will build a small app that takes in a sentence and reverses it.

We will be building out this project in CodePen. CodePen is a popular online code editor used by many developers around the world. It is free to use and you will need to create an account first and then fork the pen to get started. Here is the CodePen starter code you will need to fork that has all of the HTML and CSS.

Prerequisites
All of the HTML and CSS has been provided for you and we will not be covering any of that in this tutorial. This tutorial assumes that you have basic working knowledge of HTML and CSS. If you are interested in learning about HTML and CSS, then I suggest you go through freeCodeCamp's Responsive Web Design Course.

Table of contents

The basic functionality behind the reverse a sentence project

When you open up the CodePen starter code, you should see this result.

We want to build an app where a user inputs a sentence and clicks on the "Reverse Sentence" button, and the reversed sentence is displayed on the screen.


If the user clicks on the "Reverse Sentence" button without providing an input, then an alert with display asking them to provide a sentence.

Topics covered

Here is the complete list of topics covered in this project. You can click on any of the topics and it will take you to an article or MDN link to learn more.

Accessing HTML elements using JavaScript

Our application has three main HTML elements that we need access to in our JavaScript file. We need access to the input which contains the value for the user's sentence. We need access to the button which will be used to display the result when clicked on. And we also need access to the empty paragraph element which will display the result.

There are a few ways to access elements using JavaScript. In this project we are going to use the getElementById method. This method will access the HTML element that matches the id that we specify. Since id names have to be unique in an HTML document, we can be certain that we are getting back the correct element.

Here is the basic syntax for the getElementById method:

document.getElementById(id);

Let's first start by accessing the input which has an id of user-sentence.

document.getElementById("user-sentence");

We want to be able to assign that to a variable so we can use it later on in our program. We are going to create a const variable called userSentence and assign it the document.getElementById("user-sentence"). We are using the const keyword here because this const variable will not be re assigned.

const userSentence = document.getElementById("user-sentence");

If you are curious on what this document.getElementById("user-sentence") returns, then I would suggest using console.log like this:

const userSentence = document.getElementById("user-sentence");
console.log(userSentence);

Note: To access the console in CodePen, click on the console button in the bottom left hand corner of the screen

You can use console.log to print messages to the console output. This method comes in handy when you need to understand what your code is doing.

Now that we understand how to use console.log, we can remove that line from our program.

The next steps would be to access the paragraph element with the id of reversed-str-result. We can use the getElementById method again and assign it to a variable called reversedStrElement.

const reversedStrElement = document.getElementById("reversed-str-result");

The last element we need access to would be the button with the id of reverse-str-btn. We can use the getElementById method again and assign it to a variable called reversedStrBtn.

const reversedStrBtn = document.getElementById("reverse-str-btn");

Your first three lines of code inside the JavaScript section should look like this:

const userSentence = document.getElementById("user-sentence");
const reversedStrElement = document.getElementById("reversed-str-result");
const reversedStrBtn = document.getElementById("reverse-str-btn");

Creating the reverse sentence button functionality

When the user clicks on the "Reverse Sentence" button, we want to display the reversed sentence on the screen. We need to start by using the addEventListener method.

Adding an event listener to the Reverse Sentence button

The addEventListener is used to detect events in your program and execute a function when that event has occurred. The event that we are listening for is the click event to represent when a user clicks on a button.

Below the const variables, let's add an event listener to our reversedStrBtn.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { // the rest of our functionality will go here
});

We can test this out by adding a console.log that will print the message "This button was clicked".

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { console.log("This button was clicked");
});

Open up your console and click on the button. You should see the following result:

Now that we know our button is working, we can go ahead and remove that console.log.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { // the rest of our functionality will go here
});

Accessing the value from the input element

We need to be able to get the user's input so we can successfully reverse it and display it on the screen. We can accomplish this by adding .value to our userSentence variable.

Inside the function for our event listener, let's add a console.log for userSentence.value.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { console.log(userSentence.value);
});

Open up your console, type in a sentence and click on the "Reverse Sentence" button to see the value in the console.

We can remove that console.log and assign that user value to a const variable called userValue.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value;
});

Adding an alert for users that do not provide an input

Before we can start reversing a string, we need to check if the user provides a value.

Below the userValue variable, we need to add an if statement. An if statement is a type of conditional statement that will run a block of code if the condition is true.

Here is the basic syntax for an if statement:


if(/*condition is true*/){ // run this code here
}

For our condition, we want to check if the user value is an empty string. Empty strings in JavaScript are considered falsey values. In if statements, falsey values are coerced to false. We want to use the ! operator which reverses the boolean value (ex. true is now false, and false is now true).

This is what our if will look like:

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (!userValue) { // run this code here if condition is true }
});

The !userValue is basically saying run this code in the if statement if userValue is not false. If you don't feel comfortable with the ! operator, then you could rewrite your if statement like this:

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (userValue === "") { // run this code here if condition is true }
});

Inside the if statement, we want to add an alert to let users know that they need to provide a sentence. Below that alert, we need to add the return statement because we want to exit our function if the user does not provide an input.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (!userValue) { alert("Please provide a sentence"); return; }
});

Click on the "Reverse Sentence" button without providing an input to see the alert.

Reversing the sentence using the split, reverse, and join methods

There are many different ways to reverse a string. In this project we are going to use three built in JavaScript methods.

The first step is to take our userValue and split that into individual characters. We can use the split method which takes in a string and creates an array of characters.

Add a console.log below our if statement for the split method.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (!userValue) { alert("Please provide a sentence"); return; } console.log(userValue.split(""));
});

Open up the console, provide an input and click on the "Reverse Sentence" button to see the console results.

Next, we want to take that array of characters and reverse it using the reverse method.
Note: I am using a method called chaining here which is used to chain multiple methods together

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (!userValue) { alert("Please provide a sentence"); return; } console.log(userValue.split("").reverse());
});

Open up the console, provide an input and click on the "Reverse Sentence" button to see the console results.

Lastly, we need to convert that array of characters back into a string. We can use the join method to accomplish this.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (!userValue) { alert("Please provide a sentence"); return; } console.log(userValue.split("").reverse().join(""));
});

Open up the console, provide an input and click on the "Reverse Sentence" button to see the console results.

We can remove the console.log and assign the reversed sentence to a const variable called reversedSentence.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (!userValue) { alert("Please provide a sentence"); return; } const reversedSentence = userValue.split("").reverse().join("");
});

Displaying the reversed sentence and working with template strings

Now that we have the reversed sentence, we want to be able to display that result on the screen. We can use what are called template strings(or template literals) to inject variables inside strings. Template strings use backticks instead of double("") or single quotes('').

Here is what our template string will look like:

`Reversed sentence: ${reversedSentence}`;

We want that value to display in the reversedStrElement which represents the empty paragraph element in our HTML document. We can use the innerText method to render the reversed sentence on the page.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (!userValue) { alert("Please provide a sentence"); return; } const reversedSentence = userValue.split("").reverse().join(""); reversedStrElement.innerText = `Reversed sentence: ${reversedSentence}`;
});

Resetting the input value after the result is displayed on the page

When the result is displayed on the page, it would be nice if the input value would clear out. We accomplish this by assigning an empty string to the userSentence.value.

reversedStrBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { const userValue = userSentence.value; if (!userValue) { alert("Please provide a sentence"); return; } const reversedSentence = userValue.split("").reverse().join(""); reversedStrElement.innerText = `Reversed sentence: ${reversedSentence}`; userSentence.value = "";
});

Now you can test out your project with a variety of sentences and should see the reversed results.

Here is the entire code for our project. Here is the final working result.

Conclusion

I hope you learned a lot with this beginner JavaScript project. For more JavaScript projects like these, please check out the following article:

  • 40 JavaScript Projects for Beginners – Easy Ideas to Get Started Coding JS
item.author: codergirl1991

item.title: 致力于 Seneca 的 VS Code 扩展 | Working on Seneca's VS Code extension
item.description:
泰穆尔列夫逊

发表于这周我正在研究我学校的 vs 代码扩展，它是开源的并托管在github 上。

概述

我决定实施的功能来自我们学校社区讨论中的某个人，其想法是制作一些功能，允许人们拥有一些“标题”，顶部有一些学生信息（某种标题）。 它必须包括学生的姓名、电子邮件、文件路径等。

如何实施

我脑子里的完美实现是 -> 用户创建文件，并且包含关于他的信息的标题自动插入到文件顶部。 不幸的是，我们生活在现实世界中，在那里我对制作 vs 代码扩展的知识几乎为零，因此作为此功能的第一次迭代，我决定只制作一个代码片段。 幸运的是，vs code 提供了一些关于为你的代码扩展创建代码片段的很好的文档，而且我还找到了一个非常有用的关于它的youtube 视频。

执行

实施本身并不是很复杂，我只需要创建带有片段的.json文件、片段本身并将片段与package.json中的项目相关联，并列出支持的编程语言。 片段本身最终看起来像这样： 

 "Student File Header Template": { "prefix": "!fhead", "body": [ "$LINE_COMMENT $RELATIVE_FILEPATH", "$LINE_COMMENT Created on: $CURRENT_MONTH_NAME $CURRENT_DATE $CURRENT_YEAR", "$LINE_COMMENT Student name: ${1:fullname}", "$LINE_COMMENT Student email: ${2:email}@myseneca.ca", "$LINE_COMMENT Course: ${3:coursename}", "$LINE_COMMENT Section: ${4:section}" ], "description": "Place file header with student info" }

测试

我发现测试它的最佳方法是这样的：
我通过复制项目目录并将其粘贴到~/.vscode/extensions中的扩展文件夹并在之后重新加载 vs 代码来手动测试它。 新创建的代码段!fhead应该可供您使用（有关支持的语言，请参阅package.json ）

公关

当我完成后，我创建了PR ，我的一些同事留下了一些评论和一些小建议，我解决了这些问题，并且 PR 获得了批准和合并。

结论

我以前从未使用过 VS 代码扩展，我发现这种体验非常有趣。 我很乐意更多地从事此类项目。

别列日涅夫弗拉基米尔 - 一旦暂停，myrfion 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，myrfion 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，myrfion 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 myrfion 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 myrfion 的操作：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 myrfion 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Tymur Levtsun

Posted on Nov 24

This week I was working on my school's vs code extension which is open-sourced and hosted on the github.

Overview

The feature I decided to implement came to somebody in the discussion in our school community and the idea was to make some functionality that will allow people to have some 'header' with some student information on the top of it (some sort of header). It had to include the student's name, email, the path to the file etc.

How to implement

The perfect implementation that I had in my head was -> user creates the file and the header with information about him is automatically inserted on the top of the file. Unfortunately, we live in the real-world where I had almost zero knowledge about making vs code extensions so as the first iteration of this feature I decided to make just a code snippet. Luckily, vs code provided some good documentation about creating code snippets for your code extension and also I find one very useful youtube video about it.

Implementation

Implementation itself wasn't very complicated and I just had to create the .json file with snippets, the snippet itself and associate the snippet with the project in package.json and list supported programming languages which will work with it. The snippet itself ended up looking like this:

"Student File Header Template": {
         "prefix": "!fhead",
         "body": [
         "$LINE_COMMENT $RELATIVE_FILEPATH",
         "$LINE_COMMENT Created on: $CURRENT_MONTH_NAME $CURRENT_DATE $CURRENT_YEAR",
         "$LINE_COMMENT Student name: ${1:fullname}",
         "$LINE_COMMENT Student email: ${2:email}@myseneca.ca",
         "$LINE_COMMENT Course: ${3:coursename}",
         "$LINE_COMMENT Section: ${4:section}"
         ],
         "description": "Place file header with student info"
         }

Testing

The best way I found to test it was like this:
I tested it manually way just by copying the project's directory and pasting it to the extensions folder in ~/.vscode/extensions and reloading vs code after that. The newly created snippet !fhead should become available for you (refer to package.json for supported languages)

PR

When I was done I created the PR, where some of my peers left some comments with some minor proposals, which I resolved and the PR got approved and merged.

Conclusion

I have never worked with vs code extensions before and I find this experience very interesting. I would love to work on this kind of project more.

Samina Rahman Purba - Oct 20

Berezhnev Vladimir - Oct 20

Gulnur Baimukhambetova - Oct 19

item.title: 设计包括、游戏、安全和比索阿伊多斯 | Design inclusivo, gamificação, segurança e pessoas idosas
item.description:
米莱娜格德斯

发表于作为 telas foram projetadas no Figma，seguindo padrões de de design centrado no usuário，principalmente os estabelecidos por Don Norman e visando tornar a applicação acessível a todos，作为 WCAG foram adotados 的指导。 开始使用 Java Script、React 库。

查津尼奥 ;)

原文:

Milena Guedes

Posted on Nov 24

Em meados de julho desse ano tive a oportunidade de apresentar a pesquisa realizada por aproximadamente 1 ano e meio. Ela foi intitulada como: A pessoa idosa e a segurança na internet - Design Inclusivo aplicado no desenvolvimento de uma ferramenta gamificada que estimula o uso seguro da internet. O objetivo da pesquisa foi desenvolver uma gamificação baseada nos princípios de design inclusivo, que tem como objetivo estimular pessoas idosas a utilizarem a internet de forma segura.

As telas foram projetadas no Figma, seguindo padrões de design centrado no usuário, principalmente os estabelecidos por Don Norman e visando tornar a aplicação acessível a todos, as diretrizes do WCAG foram adotados. Para o desenvolvimento foi utilizada a biblioteca Java Script, React.

O mais legal foi poder estudar diversos assuntos e conecta-los na pesquisa, além disso, ao final do desenvolvimento a aplicação foi testada por pessoas idosas e obteve um percentual de aceitação e aprovação bem significativo por esses usuários! :)

Você pode conferir aqui a monografia completa.

Você pode conferir o resultado final aqui (a experiência fica melhor se você acessar por meio de um dispositivo móvel, já que o objetivo era o desenvolvimento em telas menores).

Repositório no Github

Obrigada por ler até o final! Tchauzinho ;)

item.title: Monokle、Helm 和优质 Kubernetes 部署 | Monokle, Helm & Quality Kubernetes Deployments
item.description:
在云原生时代之前，我们从 Digital Ocean 教程构建基础设施。 开发人员和 IT 团队负责为各个服务器提供在 Web 上运行服务或应用程序所需的所有硬件、网络、操作系统配置和服务。 这意味着要按照预先批准的且极其繁琐的步骤安装和配置 Nginx Web 服务器或 MySQL 数据库数十次或数百次。

最终，DevOps 出现并在精通技术的组织中站稳了脚跟，这也导致了从原始 Linux 管理向供应/配置管理和自动化工具（如 Ansible 和 Puppet）的发展。 这是朝着正确方向迈出的重要一步，但这些团队很快开始寻找比通过配置文件管理多个节点更快、更可靠的方法将他们的应用程序部署到网络上。

但现在，在云原生时代，基础设施即代码 (IaC) 不仅是最佳实践，而且也是预期的，Kubernetes 开发人员不应该重复地重新创建他们的基础设施，对吧？

不幸的是，我们陷入了同样的陷阱。 随着 Kubernetes 集群的复杂性激增，DevOps 工程师发现他们自己为常用资源（如 Web 服务器或数据库）重新创建清单的频率比他们希望的要高。 一旦他们在多个地方复制了这些资产，到处进行小的调整，他们的清单就不再是可靠的真相来源。

更复杂的是，大多数添加版本控制的尝试——现代 DevOps 工具包中的必要步骤——都没有成功。 在这个行业继续倾向于使用 GitOps 进行自动化部署的时代，可靠的版本控制已成为不可或缺的必备条件。

如果 Kubernetes 开发社区有一个包管理器——比如适用于 macOS 的 Homebrew 或适用于某些 Linux 版本的 apt——来发现、编辑和维护 Kubernetes 清单和部署会怎么样？

团队如何在 Kubernetes 部署中利用 Helm

查找并使用打包为 Helm Charts 的流行软件在​​ Kubernetes 中运行

  • 将您自己的应用程序共享为 Helm Charts
  • 创建可重现的 Kubernetes 应用程序构建
  • 智能管理您的 Kubernetes 清单文件
  • 管理 Helm 包的发布

    • 总的来说，Helm 为基于 Kubernetes 的组织提供了巨大的好处，这些组织渴望加快围绕创建功能和有效集群所需资源的工作流程。 他们可以使用 Helm 来部署标准模板，并将自定义设置置于顶层，而不是重复重写相同的公共资源或管理清单的多个版本。

    与利用本地存储的基础和补丁 YAML 文件的 Kustomize 不同，Helm 最常用于从Artifact Hub等公共存储库安装 Charts。 例如，Helm 将安装kube-prometheus-stack的过程简化为三个命令：

     helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts helm repo update helm install [RELEASE_NAME] prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack

    使用 Helm 图表有哪些挑战？

    安装三千行 Kubernetes 清单 YAML 的三个命令。 尽管 Helm 在“部署时间”方面创造了所有优势，但在抽象、可见性和调试方面可能会遇到一些令人头疼的问题。

    抽象：Helm 图表是一组复杂的规则、配置和资源，但 CLI 工具和公共存储库使其非常容易部署到您的生产环境——无论好坏。 在其默认设置中， kube-prometheus-stack有近 3,000 行 YAML 代码——1000X 抽象如何？

    可见性：大多数使用 Helm 的开发人员和 DevOps 工程师将在不阅读任何一行 YAML 的情况下部署资源和服务——这可能会非常自由或非常令人沮丧，具体取决于部署的方式。 而且 Helm 并没有让查看和分析它默认部署的清单和模板变得特别容易。 您最好的选择是手动运行helm show values ，它只会为您提供图片的默认部分。

    调试：对于大多数工作流程，了解您正在处理的清单和资源类型的唯一方法是使用helm install --dry-run --debug运行 Helm，并希望您从生成的清单文件中获得一些有意义的信息。 只是一个额外的（通常是不必要的）步骤会阻碍开发速度。

    定制：Helm 图表包含一个values.yaml文件，其中包含可用于定制的参数。 如果开发人员想要增加灵活性， values.yaml文件可能会非常复杂，但它并不完全灵活：文件中未包含的任何内容都不可参数化。 您想为广告连播添加标签以进行成本管理吗？ 运气不好，除非图表已经包含它。

    通过将 Kubernetes 配置管理和版本控制分层到围绕复杂 Kubernetes 清单的构建、优化和故障排除的现有流程中，Monokle 可帮助您更快、更无差错地进行部署。

    我们构建的 Monokle Desktop 是 Helm 图表的完美伴侣——它的配置管理和当前与未来集群状态的丰富差异解决了您使用 Helm 的所有核心问题，同时保留了您有效工作所需的精简和简化。 首先是对创建、分析、调试和部署 Helm 图表的原生支持。

    导航您的 Helm 图表

    当您将包含 Helm 图表的文件夹添加到 Monokle 时，它​​会立即组织您的Chart.yaml 、 values.yaml和模板文件，如上面的屏幕截图所示。 您可以在编辑器中查看和编辑任何选定资源、文件或模板的源代码。

    由于 Monokle 了解您的 Helm 模板及其对值文件中提供的属性的使用，因此您可以交互地识别模板中引用的属性的定义位置：

    相应地将鼠标悬停在值文件中的属性将向您显示使用它的模板：

    预览和调试 Helm 图表

    单击任何values.yaml文件旁边的Preview按钮以在所选文件上运行 Helm 并在Navigator中显示生成的资源。 选择一个资源以在编辑器中显示其生成的 YAML，并使用每个资源旁边的弹出窗口来探索它如何与传入/传出链接两侧的其他资源交互。

    通过出现在生成资源之前或之后的传入/传出链接图标，您可以快速了解部署、作业、pod 等是如何相互连接的。

    您当然可以在 Helm Chart 中编辑任何文件或模板并重新运行预览，这有助于您立即了解您的更改如何影响生成的 YAML 清单，并最终影响您的 Kubernetes 集群。

    这是在受控、高度可见的环境中进行有意义的更改或执行调试的完美方式。 在部署到生产环境或与他人共享您的 Helm 图表之前捕获错误。

    您可以选择要使用的文件（以及使用顺序），在helm template和helm install之间进行选择，并设置其他环境变量。 然后 Monokle 会向您展示它将运行的完整命令，以创建预览以获得完全可见性。

    示例：使用 Helm 定义发布

    几个月前，您部署了 Web 应用程序的 v1.0，其中包含 20 个 Kubernetes 资源。 您在 Helm 图表中定义了所有内容，包括一些清单，这些清单用作多个资源的模板，这些资源需要一些小的调整，具体取决于您是部署到暂存还是生产。

    在此过程中，Monokle Desktop 的 IDE 功能（如资源验证和资源表单编辑器）帮助您快速编写新代码并防止任何基本的 YAML 语法错误。

    但现在您认为您已准备好启动 v2.0，现在是将 Monokle 置于预览模式以直观地探索不断变化的关系和生成的清单文件的最佳时机。

    为什么这是重要的一步？ 由于一些新的效率，您添加到图表中，您的 Web 应用程序现在只需要 15 个 Kubernetes 资源。

    您确信它有效，但这是一个巨大的变化。 借助 Monokle 的 Helm 预览和资源导航工具，您可以完全了解图表的内部工作情况，但不必扫描多个 YAML 文件或在 CLI 上进行空运行来调试输出。

    您甚至可以单击Diff按钮来可视化特定资源将如何部署到集群的变化。

    您需要有信心将 v2.0 推送到您的版本控制中，并通过您的 CI/CD 管道证明您可以在第一时间交付功能强大的 Kubernetes 集群。

    我们正在添加对模板、存储库管理和许多其他功能的支持，这些功能将帮助您轻松、快速且自信地创建自己的 Helm 图表。

    加入对话并通过建议可使您的工作更轻松的功能来帮助我们。

    用 Monokle 掌舵

    要开始使用，请跳转到我们的下载页面以获取适用于 macOS、Windows 或 Linux 的 Monokle。 如果您还没有安装 Helm ，您也应该从那里开始。

    无论您是开发新的 Helm 图表以与 Kubernetes 社区分享，还是修改您从 Artifact Hub 获得的图表，我们都乐于了解您使用我们的 Manifest IDE 的体验。 在Discord上加入我们，分享有关您的工作流程的详细信息，并与其他渴望帮助 Helm 爱好者更好地大规模管理清单的 Kubernetes 开发人员聊天。



    原文:

    Before the cloud native era, we built infrastructure from Digital Ocean tutorials. Developers and IT teams were responsible for provisioning individual servers with all the hardware, networking, OS configurations, and services necessary to run services or apps on the web. That meant installing and configuring an Nginx web server or MySQL database dozens or hundreds of times following pre-approved—and extremely tedious—steps.

    Eventually, DevOps emerged and took hold among technology-savvy organizations, which also led to a progression away from raw Linux administration and toward provisioning/configuration management and automation tools like Ansible and Puppet. That was a major step in the right direction, but these teams quickly started to look for faster and more reliable ways to deploy their apps to the web than administering multiple nodes via configuration files.

    But now, in the cloud native era, where infrastructure as code (IaC) is not only best practice but also expected, Kubernetes developers shouldn’t have to recreate their infrastructure repeatedly, right?

    Unfortunately, we’ve fallen into the same trap. As Kubernetes clusters ballooned in complexity, DevOps engineers found themselves recreating manifests for commonly-needed resources, like web servers or databases, more often than they would like. And as soon as they replicated those assets in multiple places, making minor tweaks here and there, their manifests were no longer reliable sources of truth.

    And to complicate matters more, most attempts to add version control—a necessary step in modern DevOps toolkits, were unsuccessful. In an era where the industry continues to trend toward automating deployments with GitOps, reliable version control has become a non-negotiable must-have.

    What if the Kubernetes development community had a package manager—like Homebrew for macOS or apt for certain flavors of Linux—for discovering, editing, and maintaining Kubernetes manifests and deployments?

    How teams leverage Helm in Kubernetes deployments

    Helm is a tool for managing charts, which are packages of pre-configured Kubernetes resources. Charts contain a description of the package in the Chart.yaml file, plus one or more templates containing Kubernetes manifest files in YAML.

    Kubernetes developers and DevOps teams are already using Helm for lots of robust use cases, pulled straight from the Helm README on GitHub:

    • Find and use popular software packaged as Helm Charts to run in Kubernetes
    • Share your own applications as Helm Charts
    • Create reproducible builds of your Kubernetes applications
    • Intelligently manage your Kubernetes manifest files
    • Manage releases of Helm packages

    Overall, Helm provides great benefit for Kubernetes-based organizations eager to speed up their workflows around creating the resources needed for a functional and effective cluster. Instead of rewriting the same common resources repeatedly or mismanaging multiple versions of a manifest, they can use Helm to deploy standard templates with their customizations layered on top.

    Unlike Kustomize, which utilizes a locally-stored base and patches YAML files, Helm is most commonly used to install Charts from a public repository like Artifact Hub. For example, Helm simplifies the process of installing the kube-prometheus-stack, which deploys end-to-end Kubernetes cluster monitoring with Prometheus, into just three commands:

    helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install [RELEASE_NAME] prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack

    What are the challenges of working with Helm charts?

    Three commands to install three thousand lines of Kubernetes manifest YAML. For all the advantages Helm creates in terms of “time to deployment,” there are a few headaches that may be experienced in abstraction, visibility, and debugging.

    Abstraction: Helm charts are complex groups of rules, configurations, and resources, but the CLI tooling and public repositories make it remarkably easy to deploy to your production environment—for better or worse. In its default settings, kube-prometheus-stack has nearly 3,000 lines of YAML code—how’s that for 1000X abstraction?

    Visibility: Most developers and DevOps engineers using Helm will deploy resources and services without ever reading a single line of YAML—which can be extremely freeing or monstrously frustrating, depending on how the deployment goes. And Helm doesn’t make it particularly easy to view and analyze the manifests and templates it deploys by default. Your best option is to manually run helm show values, which only gives you the default part of the picture.

    Debugging: For most workflows, the only way to understand what kind of manifests and resources you’re dealing with is by running Helm with helm install --dry-run --debug and hoping you get some meaningful information from the resulting manifest file. Just one extra (and often unnecessary) step that gets in the way of development velocity.

    Customization: Helm charts include a values.yaml file that includes the parameters available for customization. The values.yaml file can be quite complicated if the developer wants to add flexibility, but it is not totally flexible: anything not included in the file won’t be parameterizable. Do you want to add a label to the pods for cost management? Bad luck unless the chart already includes it.

    How does Monokle Desktop help with deploying to Kubernetes via Helm?

    Monokle Desktop is a cross-platform desktop app that helps DevOps teams and developers define, compare, and audit the desired and actual states of their Kubernetes cluster. By layering Kubernetes configuration management and version control into your existing processes around building, optimizing, and troubleshooting complex Kubernetes manifests, Monokle helps you deploy faster and error-free.

    And we’ve built Monokle Desktop to be the perfect accompaniment to Helm charts—its configuration management and rich diffs on current vs. future cluster state solves all your core problems around using Helm while retaining the streamlining and simplification you need to work effectively. That starts with native support for creating, analyzing, debugging, and deploying Helm charts.

    Navigate your Helm charts

    When you add a folder with Helm charts to Monokle, it immediately organizes your Chart.yaml, values.yaml, and template files as shown in the screenshot above. You can see and edit the source code for any selected resource, file or template in the Editor.

    Since Monokle understands your Helm templates and their usage of properties provided in your values files, you can interactively identify where properties referenced in your templates are defined:

    And correspondingly hovering a property in a values file will show you which template(s) that use it:

    Monokle will also help you identify any property references that might be invalid - and since all validation is in real-time you can edit your templates and get corresponding feedback that your errors are fixed accordingly.

    Preview and debug Helm charts

    Click the Preview button next to any values.yaml file to run Helm on the selected file and show the generated resources in the Navigator. Select a resource to show its generated YAML in the Editor, and use the popups next to each resource to explore how it interacts with others on either side of an incoming/outgoing link.

    Through the incoming/outgoing links icons that appear before or after your generated resources, you can quickly understand how the deployments, jobs, pods, and more are interconnected.

    You can of course edit any file or template in your Helm Chart and re-run the preview, which helps you immediately understand how your changes impact the generated YAML manifest and, ultimately, your Kubernetes cluster.

    It’s a perfect way to make meaningful changes or perform debugging in a controlled, highly-visible environment. Catch bugs before deploying to production or share your Helm chart with others.

    Monokle’s Helm preview feature is also customizable—you can even have multiple preview configurations based on your needs. You can select which files to use (and in what order), choose between helm template and helm install, and set other environment variables. Monokle then shows you the complete command it’ll run to create the preview for full visibility.

    Example: Defining releases with Helm

    A few months ago, you deployed v1.0 of your web application, which consisted of 20 Kubernetes resources. You defined everything in a Helm chart, including a few manifests that serve as templates for multiple resources that require a few small tweaks depending on whether you’re deploying to staging or production.

    After the v1.0 release, you immediately got started on v2.0. Along the way, Monokle Desktop’s IDE features, like resource validation and the resource form editor, helped you quickly write new code and prevent any basic YAML syntax errors.

    But now that you think you’re ready to launch v2.0, it’s the perfect time to put Monokle into Preview mode to explore the changing relationships and the generated manifest files visually.

    Why is this an important step? Because of some new efficiencies, you added to your chart, your web app now only needs 15 Kubernetes resources.

    You’re confident that it works, but it’s a dramatic change. With Monokle’s Helm preview and resource navigation tools, you have full visibility into your chart’s inner workings, but you don’t have to scan multiple YAML files or do dry runs on the CLI to debug the output.

    You can even click the Diff button to visualize changes in how specific resources will get deployed to your cluster.

    It’s the confidence you need to push v2.0 into your version control and prove, through your CI/CD pipeline, that you can deliver a powerfully functional Kubernetes cluster the first time.

    Next steps for Monokle

    Monokle Desktop roadmap is full of additions to our functional support for Helm. We are adding support for templates, repo management, and many other features that will help you create your own Helm charts easily, fast, and with confidence.

    Join the conversation and help us by suggesting functions that will make your job easier.

    Take the Helm with Monokle

    To get started, hop over to our downloads page to get Monokle for macOS, Windows, or Linux. If you don’t already have Helm installed, you should start there as well.

    Whether you’re developing new Helm charts to share with the Kubernetes community or modifying the charts you get from Artifact Hub, we’d love to hear about your experiences with our Manifest IDE. Join us on Discord to share details about your workflows and chat with other Kubernetes developers who are eager to help their Helm-loving comrades better manage manifests at scale.
item.author: cortney

item.title: 免费课程 - 学习：使用 Azure SQL 和 Prisma 的全栈应用程序 | Free Course - Learn: A Full Stack Application with Azure SQL and Prisma
item.description:

    本课程是与 Azure SQL 产品团队和 Prisma DevRel 团队共同创建的。 在这种情况下，Davide Mauri - 首席项目经理 Azure SQL和Alex Ruheni - Developer Advocate Prisma

    该课程包含 37 个视频。 并且，它分为6个模块：

    如果您想⭐并与您的朋友分享，请这样做！ 我们将万分感激！

    培训资料库如下：

    并且，您可以在下面看到课程播放列表：

    最后的话

    我希望这个免费课程Learn: A Full Stack Application with Azure SQL & Prisma对你有用。
    啊! 我差点忘了！ 完成本课程后，您可以查看以下 Microsoft Learn 模块：

    如果您想了解更多有关 JavaScript、TypeScript 和 Azure 的知识，请不要忘记在 Twitter 上关注我： @glaucia_lemos86 ！ 并且，我们下一篇文章再见！ 这篇帖子在 2020 年的 DEV 上火了：

    作为 JavaScript 开发者，我们通常不必自己处理编译器。 然而，了解 JavaScript 引擎的基础知识并了解它如何处理我们对人类友好的 JS 代码并将其转化为机器可以理解的代码绝对是一件好事！ 编码愉快！

穆罕默德·阿兹法尔·阿斯拉姆 - 一旦暂停，Azure 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

原文:

Hi, Coders! 👋

How long, isn't it? 😅 I'm back with a new article, and with a great return! 🎉
Today I want to share with you a complete free course, with over 37 videos teaching you how to create a complete Full Stack application using:

  • ✅ Node.js
  • ✅ Vue.Js
  • ✅ Prisma
  • ✅ Azure SQL
  • ✅ Azure Functions
  • ✅ Azure Static Web Apps
  • ✅ GitHub Actions
  • ✅ DevContainer

Yup. An E2E (End to End) application from Front to Back-End (However, with a focus on development on the Back-End side).

This course was co-created with the Azure SQL Product Team and the Prisma DevRel Team. In this case Davide Mauri - Principal Program Manager Azure SQL and Alex Ruheni - Developer Advocate Prisma

📚 Course Program

The course contains 37 videos. And, it is divided into 6 modules:

📹 Module 01: Prisma Fundamentals 📹 Module 02: Azure SQL Fundamentals 📹 Module 03: Azure Static Web Apps Fundamentals 📹 Module 04: Dev Tools 📹 Module 05: Application Development/Deployment

📹 Module 06: Next Steps

📺 Course Videos

If you want to ⭐ and share with your friends, please do! We will be extremely grateful!

The training repository is below:

And, below you can see the course playlist:

📹 Complete Playlist: HERE

Final Words

I hope this free course Learn: A Full Stack Application with Azure SQL & Prisma could be useful for you.
Ah! I was almost forgetting! After to finish this course, you can take a look at the Microsoft Learn modules below:

✅ Build serverless, full stack applications in Azure - Free Course
✅ Azure SQL Documentation
✅ Microsoft Learn: Free Course - Azure Static Web Apps
✅ Microsoft Learn: Free Course - Build Serverless APIs with Azure Functions

✅ Prisma Official Website


✅ Official Prisma Documentation
✅ Articles & Updates about Prisma
✅ Prisma Community
✅ Prisma Youtube Channel

And if you want to learn more stuff about JavaScript, TypeScript with Azure don't forget to follow me on Twitter: @glaucia_lemos86! There's a lot of contents coming up in 2023 😃!

And, see you all in the next article! 🤗

This post blew up on DEV in 2020:

🚀⚙️ JavaScript Visualized: the JavaScript Engine

As JavaScript devs, we usually don't have to deal with compilers ourselves. However, it's definitely good to know the basics of the JavaScript engine and see how it handles our human-friendly JS code, and turns it into something machines understand! 🥳

Happy coding!

Muhammad Azfar Aslam - Nov 1

item.title: 一起写故事吧。 | | "Novel", a collaborative story-telling “小说”，协作讲故事 | Let's write stories together. | "Novel", a collaborative story-telling
item.description:
瓦希德阿尔

发表于嗨伙计，
希望你做得很好。

最近我有一个想法，想做一个平台，让人们可以一起创作东西，因为我喜欢写作，所以我决定创作“小说” 。 一个像你这样的人一起写故事的平台，一部分一部分。

当您打开 Novel 时，您可以找到其他人创建的故事，您可以在这些故事上进行协作，甚至可以开始您自己的故事并让其他人加入您的行列。

如果您发现任何问题，或者您对如何改进有任何想法，请告诉我。

保重，下期见！

一旦暂停，thevahidal 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，thevahidal 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，thevahidal 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 thevahidal 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 thevahidal：

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 thevahidal 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Vahid Al

Posted on Nov 24

Hi folks,
hope you're doing well.

Recently I had this idea for a platform where people can create stuff together, and since I like writing, I decided to create "Novel". A platform where folks like you write an story together, part by part.

When you open Novel, you can find stories create by others that you can collaborate on them, or even start your own story and let others join you on continuing it.

Novel is open-source so you can checkout its source code and contribute on making it if you like.

Please let me know if you find any issues, or if you have ideas on how to improve it.

Take care, and I see you in the next one!

Vincent A. Cicirello - Nov 23
